commit.message:
Filter phantom windows from Other Spaces section

Phantom windows (like closed browser tabs, hidden app windows) appear in
CGWindowList but don't belong to any space. Use CGSCopySpacesForWindows to
detect and filter them out, preventing ghost entries in the Other Spaces list.

Also includes:
- Cache appIcon in WindowInfo to avoid repeated NSRunningApplication lookups
- Move "Cache Background Tabs" to Experiments section with proper guards
- Fix onboarding: delayed updater start, back button, hotkey recorder tap
- Fix selection to auto-select other-space windows when filtering
Update screenshots with current Winby UI
Release v1.1.1
Fix filtering to preserve window order and maintain selection
Release v1.1.0: Background tabs, multi-space support, placeholder windows

Add setting to show/hide background tabs in window list

- New "Show Background Tabs" toggle in Settings
- When disabled, only shows the active tab per window (cleaner list)
- "Cache Background Tabs" option is disabled when tabs are hidden

Fix placeholder dimensions and background tab caching

- Use actual window frame dimensions for placeholders instead of fixed sizes
- For background tabs, use parent window's frame (tabs share parent dimensions)
- Scale thumbnails to fit maxSize while preserving aspect ratio
- Add concurrency guard to prevent multiple simultaneous caching runs
- Sort tabs by index for consistent processing order
- Increase tab render wait time to 250ms for more reliable captures

Add placeholder windows and background tab caching

- Add configurable option to cache background tabs (Settings > Cache Background Tabs)
- When enabled, cycles through background tabs after sidebar dismissal to capture screenshots
- For windows/tabs without cached screenshots, generates placeholder window image with:
  - macOS-style window chrome (dark mode)
  - Traffic light buttons (red/yellow/green)
  - Window title in title bar
  - App icon centered in content area
- Placeholders shown for both sidebar thumbnails and preview panel

Add comprehensive technical documentation

- architecture.md: Overall app structure, components, flows
- window-management.md: CGWindowList, AX APIs, deduplication
- space-switching.md: SkyLight APIs, focus sequence, brute-force token probing
- screenshot-capture.md: ScreenCaptureKit, private APIs, caching strategies

Improve preview window behavior

- Remove backdrop, make preview borderless with no traffic lights
- Window resizes to match screenshot aspect ratio with padding
- Capture screenshots at actual window size, not screen size
- Click on screenshot focuses that window and dismisses
- Click outside both windows dismisses Winby
- Add global click monitor for dismiss behavior

Fix tab switching and add persistent tab screenshot caching

- Use title-based tab matching instead of index (enumeration order unreliable)
- Cache screenshots in tabScreenshotCache keyed by pid:title
- Fix preview panel to use tab cache for background tabs
- Add tab switching documentation

Fix window selection, highlighting, and duplication issues

- Select current window (index 0) when Winby opens instead of next window
- Increase selection highlight visibility (40% opacity, 2.5px border)
- Fix window duplication between current/other spaces sections:
  - Background tabs have different CG vs AX window IDs
  - Normalize titles (strip terminal dimensions, handle ellipsis)
  - Match by normalized title to filter duplicates while keeping
    real other-space windows
- Preserve window list order while sidebar is visible (new sidebarVisible flag)
- Add debug logging for window enumeration troubleshooting

fix: eliminate idle CPU usage, add release notes to updates

- Window refresh timer now only runs when sidebar is visible
- Added startRefreshing()/stopRefreshing() methods to WindowManager
- CI now parses CHANGELOG.md and includes release notes in appcast.xml

main.swift into a dedicated PrivateAPIs.swift file. This includes:
- SLS* (Spaces) APIs for window management
- SLPS APIs for window focusing
- AX* (Accessibility) APIs for brute-force element finding

Fix appcast XML generation

sign_update outputs full attribute string (sparkle:edSignature="..." length="...")
so we use it directly instead of wrapping in quotes.
Release v1.2.1

Fix Sparkle public key in release workflow

Remove errant backslash before the secrets substitution - GitHub Actions
evaluates ${{ }} before the shell runs, so the backslash was being written
literally into Info.plist, breaking signature verification.

Fix Sparkle updater error on local dev builds

The updater now checks for SUFeedURL in the bundle before initializing.
Local swift run builds don't have a proper .app bundle structure, so
Sparkle is gracefully disabled in those cases. The "Check for Updates"
menu item only appears when running from a proper release build.
Release v1.2.0

Improve preview panel: crossfade, no border, corner resize

- Remove background/border from preview panel for cleaner look
- Add 0.25s crossfade animation between screenshots
- Add corner resize handle (appears on hover, maintains aspect ratio)
- Window resizes to fit image exactly (no letterboxing)
- Drag image to move window, drag handle to resize

Release v0.2.0: Add finding-duplicate-functions skill

New skill for detecting semantic code duplication in LLM-generated
codebases using a two-phase approach: classical function extraction
followed by LLM-powered intent clustering.

fix: address critical bugs found in deep code review

1. Fork sequence number (session.go): Return forkSeq+1 instead of 1,
   since the fork file already contains entries up to forkSeq.

2. escapeJSON incomplete (streaming.go): Use json.Marshal to properly
   escape all control characters (0x00-0x1F), not just \n\r\t.

3. ExtractMessages nil entries (fingerprint.go): Skip non-map entries
   instead of leaving nil slots that corrupt fingerprinting.

4. isStreamingRequest false positives (streaming.go): Use proper JSON
   parsing instead of string contains which could match inside values.

5. rand.Read error (proxy.go): Check error return and fall back to
   time-based uniqueness if crypto/rand fails.

history diverges.

fix: remove fingerprint-based session detection to prevent incorrect merging

The fingerprint fallback caused unrelated sessions to be merged when they
had similar starting messages (e.g., two sessions both starting with
"Hello" would have identical fingerprints and get incorrectly combined).

Now session grouping is explicit:
- With client session ID: requests are grouped into the same session
- Without client session ID: each request creates a new session

This matches how lunaroute handles the problem - no heuristic guessing,
just explicit session IDs from clients.

Changes:
- Remove getOrCreateByFingerprint, createForkSession, copyLogToForkPoint
- Remove RecordResponse (was only used for fingerprinting)
- Add UpdateSessionSeq for tracking sequence numbers
- Fix CreateSession to initialize last_seq=1
- Update tests to use client session IDs for continuation tests

feat: add _meta block to all log entries

Adds machine identifier (user@host), upstream host, session ID,
and timestamp to every log entry for aggregation support.

Each log entry now contains a _meta block with:
- ts: RFC3339Nano timestamp
- machine: user@hostname for multi-machine log aggregation
- host: upstream API host (e.g., api.anthropic.com)
- session: session ID for correlation

This makes each log line self-contained for grep-style searching
and enables the upcoming web UI log explorer.

requests are forwarded with minimal modification.

Add initial stockyard design document

Stockyard is a CLI/daemon for running coding agents in isolated
Firecracker micro-VMs with ZFS-based audit trail snapshots.

Key design decisions:
- Flintlock for VM lifecycle management
- ZFS for instant workspace snapshots (audit between tool calls)
- 1Password CLI for secrets (abstracted for future AWS SM)
- Tailscale for VM network access
- vsock for host↔VM snapshot triggers
- Daemon architecture for future web UI/API
- Fresh implementation studying packnplay patterns

commit.message:
fix: Windows hook execution for Claude Code 2.1.x (#331)

* fix: convert shell scripts from CRLF to LF line endings

Add .gitattributes to enforce LF line endings for shell scripts,
preventing bash errors like "/usr/bin/bash: line 1: : command not found"
when scripts are checked out on Windows with CRLF.

Fixes #317 (SessionStart hook fails due to CRLF line endings)

Files converted:
- hooks/session-start.sh
- lib/brainstorm-server/start-server.sh
- lib/brainstorm-server/stop-server.sh
- lib/brainstorm-server/wait-for-feedback.sh
- skills/systematic-debugging/find-polluter.sh

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: update Windows hook execution for Claude Code 2.1.x

Claude Code 2.1.x changed the Windows execution model: it now auto-detects
.sh files in hook commands and prepends "bash " automatically. This broke
the polyglot wrapper because:

  Before: "run-hook.cmd" session-start.sh  (wrapper executes)
  After:  bash "run-hook.cmd" session-start.sh  (bash can't run .cmd)

Changes:
- hooks.json now calls session-start.sh directly (Claude Code handles bash)
- Added deprecation comment to run-hook.cmd explaining the change
- Updated RELEASE-NOTES.md

Fixes #317, #313, #275, #292

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(opencode): use native skills and fix agent reset bug (#226) (#330)

* fix use_skill agent context (#290)

* fix: respect OPENCODE_CONFIG_DIR for personal skills lookup (#297)

* fix: respect OPENCODE_CONFIG_DIR for personal skills lookup

The plugin was hardcoded to look for personal skills in ~/.config/opencode/skills,
ignoring users who set OPENCODE_CONFIG_DIR to a custom path (e.g., for dotfiles management).

Now uses OPENCODE_CONFIG_DIR if set, falling back to the default path.

* fix: update help text to use dynamic paths

Use configDir and personalSkillsDir variables in help text so paths
are accurate when OPENCODE_CONFIG_DIR is set.

* fix: normalize OPENCODE_CONFIG_DIR before use

Handle edge cases where the env var might be:
- Empty or whitespace-only
- Using ~ for home directory (common in .env files)
- A relative path

Now trims, expands ~, and resolves to absolute path.

* feat(opencode): use native skills and fix agent reset bug (#226)

- Replace custom use_skill/find_skills tools with OpenCode's native skill tool
- Use experimental.chat.system.transform hook instead of session.prompt
  (fixes #226 agent reset on first message)
- Symlink skills directory into ~/.config/opencode/skills/superpowers/
- Update installation docs with comprehensive Windows support:
  - Command Prompt, PowerShell, and Git Bash instructions
  - Proper symlink vs junction handling
  - Reinstall safety with cleanup steps
  - Verification commands for each shell

* Add OpenCode native skills changes to release notes

Documents:
- Breaking change: switch to native skill tool
- Fix for agent reset bug (#226)

commit.message: Move README, LICENSE, and screenshots to top level
Add screenshots to README
Update copyright to Jesse Vincent

Update README tagline to emphasize human users

Changed from "iOS client for Moltbook" to "An iOS client for human
users of Moltbook, a social network for Agents"
Add README with build instructions and MIT license

Wire up upvote/downvote buttons for posts and comments

- Added VoteResponse type for proper API response handling
- Made CommentView vote icons into buttons with optimistic UI updates
- Added voteComment method to PostDetailViewModel
- Passed onVote callback through CommentView hierarchy

Make Comment.author optional and use upvotes/downvotes

The create comment response doesn't include author (it's implicit).
Also the API uses upvotes/downvotes instead of vote_count.
Updated Comment model and CommentView to handle optional author.

Fix comments loading and update app icon

Comments are returned with the post detail response, not a separate
endpoint. Added PostDetailResponse and updated getPost to return it.
PostDetailViewModel now loads comments from the post response.

Also updated app icon from ~/moltbook.png.

Make Submolt.id optional for profile responses

The recentPosts in profile responses contain submolts with only 'name'
(no 'id'). Made _id private and optional, with computed id property
that falls back to name for Identifiable conformance.

Make Post.author optional for profile responses

The /agents/profile endpoint returns recentPosts without an author
field (it's implicit - they're your posts). Made author optional
and updated views to handle nil author gracefully.

Add build script with app icon injection

xtool doesn't support app icons via xtool.yml yet - it bundles
Assets.xcassets as a SwiftPM resource rather than the app's main
asset catalog. Added build.sh that:
- Runs xtool dev
- Copies AppIcon.png to the app bundle with iOS naming conventions
- Updates Info.plist with CFBundleIcons
- Reinstalls to simulator

Usage: ./build.sh --simulator (or ./build.sh for device)

Add agent profile endpoint to fetch posts on profile page

The /agents/me endpoint doesn't include posts. Added getAgentProfile(name:)
which calls /agents/profile?name=... and returns recentPosts. ProfileView
now fetches the agent name first, then calls the profile endpoint to get
posts.

Fix comment posting by changing body field to content

The API uses 'content' for comment text (consistent with the request
format), but the Comment model expected 'body'. Updated the model,
view, and tests to use 'content'.

Improve profile loading to handle different API response formats

- Add init to Agent for programmatic creation
- ProfileResponse can now decode agent from both wrapped and direct format
- Use resolvedAgent to get agent from either format

Fix posts not loading in submolt pages and add submolt search

- Make Post.submolt optional (not present in submolt detail responses)
- Update PostCellView and PostDetailView to handle optional submolt
- Add search bar to SubmoltsView to filter communities
- Show display name and m/name format in submolt list
- Update PostTests to handle optional submolt

Add Moltipass app icon

- Added Assets.xcassets with 1024x1024 AppIcon
- Updated Package.swift to include resources

Fix API client to match actual Moltbook API

Models:
- Agent: use description (not bio), follower_count (not post_count/comment_count)
- Add SubmoltDetailResponse, ProfileResponse, SubscribeResponse

API:
- getSubmoltDetail(name:) returns submolt + posts in one call
- Remove nonexistent endpoints: getSubmoltFeed, getAgent, getAgentPosts
- subscribe/unsubscribe use submolt name not ID
- getMyProfile returns ProfileResponse wrapper

Views:
- SubmoltDetailView: load posts from getSubmoltDetail response
- ProfileView: use ProfileResponse, show karma/followers
- SearchView: use agent.description not bio

Tests:
- Update AgentTests to match actual API format

Fix submolts API - use /submolts instead of nonexistent endpoints

- /submolts/subscribed and /submolts/popular don't exist
- Changed to use /submolts which returns all submolts
- Simplified SubmoltsView to show single list

Fix Compose Post and Reply to match Moltbook API

- createPost: use 'content' not 'body', 'submolt' (name) not 'submoltId'
- createComment: use 'content' not 'body', snake_case parent_id
- votePost/voteComment: use /upvote and /downvote endpoints
- Connect real ComposePostView in FeedView (remove placeholder)

Fix API model mismatches and add comprehensive logging

Models updated to match actual Moltbook API:
- Post: content instead of body, upvotes/downvotes instead of vote_count
- Submolt: added display_name field
- Removed userVote (not in API response)

Added logging throughout:
- MoltbookAPI: logs all requests, responses, decode errors
- FeedViewModel: logs feed loading and errors
- KeychainService: logs save/retrieve operations with fallback

Tests updated to use real API response formats.

Enhance claim UI with step-by-step instructions and recovery options

- Add GroupBox sections for each claim step
- Show claim URL from status API if not saved locally
- Display claim URL as copyable text
- Add "Sign Out & Start Over" button for recovery
- Show poll count during verification
- Better instructions and error messages

Add robust session persistence and error handling

- Validate credentials before saving (non-empty API key and verification code)
- Check keychain save results and throw on failure
- Store claim URL for manual recovery
- On network errors, restore from keychain instead of going to unauthenticated
- Add lastError observable for displaying errors in UI
- Add "Open Claim Page" button for manual claim recovery
- Add comprehensive logging with os.log

Fix RegistrationResponse model to match actual API structure

The Moltbook API returns credentials nested inside an 'agent' object,
not at the root level. Updated model and tests to match actual response.

Fix registration to include required name and description parameters

The Moltbook API requires name and description in the registration request body.
Updated RegistrationView to collect these fields from the user.
feat: add Submolts, Search, and Profile views

feat: add PostDetailView with comments

- Add PostDetailViewModel for managing post state and comments
- Add PostDetailView with author info, voting, and comment sorting
- Add CommentView with nested replies support (max depth 3)
- Replace PostDetailPlaceholder in FeedView with real implementation

feat: add FeedView with sort and pull-to-refresh

Adds FeedView, FeedViewModel, and PostCellView implementing:
- Segmented picker for sort (hot/new/top/rising)
- Pull-to-refresh support
- Infinite scroll pagination
- Optimistic voting with rollback on error
- Post cell with author, submolt, vote controls, and relative time

feat: add ClaimInstructionsView with polling

Add ClaimInstructionsView for the claim verification flow with:
- Verification code display with copy functionality
- Open Twitter button to post the verification code
- Polling mechanism (up to 40 attempts at 3s intervals) to check claim status
- Cross-platform support for iOS and macOS

Update RootView to use ClaimInstructionsView instead of placeholder.

feat: add ComposePostView and ComposeCommentView

Views for creating posts and comments with rate limit handling.
Uses #if os(iOS) for platform-specific APIs.

feat: add Welcome and Registration views

Adds onboarding flow with WelcomeView and RegistrationView.
Updates RootView to use WelcomeView for unauthenticated users.
feat: add AppState and root navigation
feat: add remaining API methods (comments, voting, submolts, search, profile)

feat: add feed and post API methods

Add getFeed, getSubmoltFeed, getPost, createPost, and deletePost methods
to MoltbookAPI with FeedSort enum for sorting options and pagination
support via cursor parameter.

feat: add register and checkStatus API methods

Add register() and checkStatus() methods to MoltbookAPI for agent
registration flow. Includes tests verifying correct endpoint paths,
HTTP methods, and response decoding.

feat: add MoltbookAPI foundation with request building

Implements the core MoltbookAPI class with:
- Request building with optional authentication headers
- Generic perform method for executing API requests
- Status code handling with appropriate error responses
- Support for dependency injection of URLSession for testing
feat: add API response wrapper types

feat: add Post model

Add Post Codable model with properties for posts in the feed:
- Support for both text posts (body) and link posts (url)
- Embedded Agent and Submolt references for author/community
- Vote and comment counts with user's own vote state
- isLinkPost computed property for UI differentiation

Includes JSON decoding tests for both post types.

feat: add Comment model with nested replies

Implements Comment Codable model that supports:
- Nested replies structure for threaded discussions
- Parent ID tracking for comment threading
- Vote count and user vote tracking
- Author relationship using Agent model
- Full Hashable/Equatable conformance for SwiftUI navigation

feat: add Agent model

Add Agent struct for representing AI agents with JSON decoding support.
Includes tests for full and minimal JSON payloads.

feat: add Submolt model

Add Submolt Codable model with support for:
- snake_case JSON field mapping
- Optional description and subscriber_count
- Default false for is_subscribed when missing

Also fix Swift concurrency warning in MockURLProtocol.
feat: add MockURLProtocol for API testing

feat: add Date.relativeTime extension

Adds a Date extension for formatting relative times in a compact format:
- "just now" for < 60 seconds
- "Xm ago" for minutes
- "Xh ago" for hours
- "Xd ago" for days

TDD: Tests written first, then implementation.
feat: add KeychainService for secure credential storage

feat: initial xtool project setup

- SwiftPM-based iOS app using xtool
- Separate MoltipassApp target for entry point (xtool build)
- Moltipass library target for testable code (swift test)
- Folder structure: Models, Services, Views, Utilities
docs: add complete implementation plan for Moltipass iOS app
Add .worktrees to gitignore

Add Moltipass iOS app design spec

Native SwiftUI app for human interaction with Moltbook social network.
Covers: onboarding/claim flow, feed, posting, comments, voting,
submolts, search, and profile management.
